SOP-MAINT-001.title: Centrifugal Pump P-101A/B — Routine Lubrication and Vibration Inspection
SOP-MAINT-001.content:
PURPOSE
Define the routine maintenance activities for HP Sep drain pumps P-101A and P-101B to prevent bearing failure and ensure continuous operation.

FREQUENCY
Every 14 days (or when vibration trend exceeds 2.0 mm/s).

SAFETY REQUIREMENTS
- Hot work not required. Cold work permit needed if pump is running.
- PPE: Safety glasses, gloves, hearing protection (>85 dB in pump room).
- Beware of hot pump casing surface (may reach 80°C).

TOOLS REQUIRED
- Grease gun with Shell Gadus S2 V220 2 (or approved equivalent)
- Calibrated vibration meter (Brüel & Kjær Type 2270 or equivalent)
- Infrared thermometer
- Torque wrench 10-50 Nm

LUBRICATION PROCEDURE
1.  Identify pump status — if running (active duty), proceed to Step 2. If on standby, start before lubrication.
2.  Clean grease nipple on Drive End bearing housing with clean rag.
3.  Pump exactly 3 shots (approx 15g) of Shell Gadus S2 V220 2 grease into DE nipple.
4.  Repeat for Non-Drive End bearing housing (2 shots, approx 10g).
5.  Do NOT over-grease — excess grease causes overheating and accelerated bearing failure.
6.  Check grease nipple is clear and accepts grease freely — if resistance felt, flag as defect.

VIBRATION MEASUREMENT
1.  Take vibration readings on DE and NDE in three planes: horizontal, vertical, axial.
2.  Record values on PM Checklist Form MNT-CHK-P101.
3.  Compare to baseline and previous readings.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (ISO 10816-3)
  Good:     0 – 2.3 mm/s
  Acceptable: 2.3 – 4.5 mm/s (increase inspection frequency to weekly)
  Alert:    4.5 – 7.1 mm/s (notify supervisor, plan replacement)
  DANGER:   >7.1 mm/s (isolate pump immediately, switch to standby)

TEMPERATURE CHECK
- Bearing housing temperature should be <70°C. If >80°C with no recent lubrication, suspect bearing failure.
- Motor winding temp (if accessible): <105°C.

FINDINGS AND REPORTING
Record all readings in CMMS. If ANY criterion is at Alert or above, raise a corrective work order immediately and notify the Maintenance Supervisor.

RELATED DOCUMENTS
P-101 Datasheet, CMMS Form MNT-CHK-P101, SOP-MAINT-002 (Bearing Replacement)

MAN-MECH-001.title: Sulzer ZE Centrifugal Pump — Maintenance Manual Extract (Bearings)
MAN-MECH-001.content:
SULZER ZE SERIES — BEARING MAINTENANCE GUIDELINES (EXTRACT)
Document Reference: SZ-ZE-MM-003-EN

BEARING SPECIFICATION
Drive End (DE):      SKF 6316-2RS1 — Single row deep groove ball bearing, 80mm bore
Non-Drive End (NDE): SKF 6312-2RS1 — Single row deep groove ball bearing, 60mm bore
Lubrication:         Shell Gadus S2 V220 2 (or equivalent ISO VG 220 NLGI 2 grease)

RELUBRICATION INTERVAL
Operating temperature <70°C: Relubricate every 2000 operating hours (approx. 12 weeks)
Operating temperature 70–90°C: Relubricate every 1000 operating hours (approx. 6 weeks)
Caution: DO NOT exceed relubrication interval. Starvation is the leading cause of bearing failure.

RELUBRICATION QUANTITY
Drive End:     Add 15g of grease (3 gun strokes with standard 5g grease gun)
Non-Drive End: Add 10g of grease (2 gun strokes)

WARNING: Over-greasing causes elevated bearing temperature and premature failure. Always use measured grease quantities.

BEARING REPLACEMENT — WHEN REQUIRED
Replace bearings when any of the following are observed:
- ISO vibration severity Zone D (>7.1 mm/s on 15-350 Hz broadband)
- Abnormal noise: grinding, clicking, or rumbling sound not present previously
- Bearing temperature sustained above 90°C
- Grease found discoloured (black or dark grey) with metallic particles on inspection

BEARING REPLACEMENT PROCEDURE (SUMMARY)
1.  De-energise and LOTO motor
2.  Remove coupling guard and disconnect coupling halves
3.  Remove bearing housing end cover bolts (8 × M12)
4.  Draw bearing housing off shaft using approved puller — do NOT use heat
5.  Press new bearing onto shaft using bearing fitting sleeve — do NOT strike inner race directly
6.  Pack bearing housing with 50% of cavity volume with fresh grease before assembly
7.  Re-assemble in reverse order
8.  Perform vibration check on first run (30-minute run-in)
9.  Re-check and re-grease after first 8 hours of operation after replacement

VIBRATION ALERT TABLE (ISO 10816-3, Group 1 machinery)
  Zone A (new):     0 – 2.3 mm/s
  Zone B (ok):      2.3 – 4.5 mm/s
  Zone C (monitor): 4.5 – 7.1 mm/s
  Zone D (danger):  >7.1 mm/s — remove from service

RELATED DOCUMENTS: Sulzer ZE Series Datasheet, Installation Manual SZ-ZE-IM-001-EN

SOP-OPS-010.title: LP Compressor K-201 — Startup After Trip Procedure
SOP-OPS-010.content:
PURPOSE
Describe the safe restart of LP Compressor K-201 following an unplanned trip, including pre-start checks to prevent repeat trip.

SCOPE
Operations Technicians OT Level 2+, in conjunction with Instrument Technician for vibration system reset.

PREREQUISITES
- Trip cause identified and cleared (see DCS first-up alarm and Operations Log)
- Compressor Maintenance Technician sign-off if mechanical trip (vibration, temperature)
- Suction scrubber V-201 level confirmed <30% before attempting restart

PRE-START CHECKS
1.  Check vibration readings on VT-201 (DE) and VT-202 (NDE) — must be <3.0 mm/s at rest
2.  Check lube oil level in reservoir — minimum 75% on sight glass
3.  Check lube oil pressure — must be >2.5 bar before main drive permit given
4.  Confirm anti-surge valve PCV-201 is OPEN (100%) — anti-surge must be open during start
5.  Confirm suction SDV-201 closed; discharge check valve functional
6.  Verify cooling system operational — confirm cooling water flow on local indicator

STARTUP SEQUENCE
Step 1:  Start lube oil pre-lube pump — allow 5 minutes of pre-lubrication
Step 2:  Reset vibration monitoring system — confirm all channels healthy
Step 3:  Open suction SDV-201 slowly (10% per minute) while monitoring suction pressure
Step 4:  Confirm suction pressure stable at design (5-8 barg) before proceeding
Step 5:  Start K-201 motor — observe speed ramp-up on DCS
Step 6:  As speed increases through minimum stable speed, begin closing PCV-201 (anti-surge) in AUTO mode
Step 7:  Allow compressor to reach 100% design speed; confirm discharge pressure building
Step 8:  Monitor vibration — if any channel exceeds 4.5 mm/s, STOP immediately; do NOT continue

SURGE AVOIDANCE
- During startup, if flow drops below minimum stable flow set point, PCV-201 will automatically open
- Do NOT attempt to manually override surge protection during startup
- If surge is detected (rapid pressure oscillations, noise from casing), trip compressor via DCS stop

NORMAL RUNNING CHECKS (First 30 minutes)
- Vibration readings every 5 minutes
- Lube oil temperature: 40-60°C; lube oil pressure >2.5 bar
- Discharge temperature <120°C
- No unusual noise or vibration

RELATED DOCUMENTS
K-201 Datasheet, Anti-surge Control Narrative NPA-CTRL-002, P&ID NPA-PID-005 Rev 6

SOP-MAINT-010.title: Produced Water Cooler E-301 — Chemical Cleaning Procedure
SOP-MAINT-010.content:
PURPOSE
Remove calcium carbonate and mixed organic scale deposits from E-301 shell & tube heat exchanger to restore heat transfer performance.

WHEN TO APPLY
This procedure is initiated when either:
(a) E-301 approach temperature exceeds design by >10°C for 7 consecutive days, or
(b) Shell-side differential pressure exceeds 2.0 bar (design: 0.8 bar), or
(c) Routine performance test confirms heat transfer coefficient Uo has degraded by >30%.

SCOPE
Chemical clean using recirculated inhibited HCl solution. Requires chemical injection contractor involvement.

SAFETY REQUIREMENTS
- Permit to Work: Cold Work + Chemical Handling Permit required
- COSHH Assessment reference: COSHH-2020-E301-HCL
- PPE: Full face shield, nitrile chemical gloves, acid-resistant apron, steel-toed boots
- Emergency eye-wash station confirmed operational within 10m
- Neutralisation vessel available for spent acid disposal
- H2S monitor worn at all times (HCl + sulphide scale can generate H2S)

PROCEDURE

Phase 1 — Pre-clean Isolation and Bypass
1.  Route process through E-301 bypass and confirm zero flow on E-301 flow meter FT-301
2.  Close and blank shell-side inlet and outlet isolation valves
3.  Fit temporary hose connections on drain/vent nozzles for recirculation loop
4.  Verify temperature on shell-side <40°C before proceeding

Phase 2 — Water Flush
1.  Fill shell-side with clean water (demineralised preferred)
2.  Circulate for 30 minutes at 1 m/s velocity
3.  Drain flush water to closed drain — note colour and pH
4.  Repeat until pH of drain water matches pH of inlet water (typically 2 flushes)

Phase 3 — Acid Circulation (Chemical Contractor activity)
1.  Prepare 15% w/w HCl solution with 0.5% Rodine 213 corrosion inhibitor in 2000L IBC
2.  Circulate at 0.8 m/s for 4 hours at ambient temperature
3.  Monitor pH of circulating solution every 30 minutes — when pH stabilises (pH rise <0.2 in 30 min), scale is dissolved
4.  Sample every hour: if iron content in solution >2000 ppm, refresh acid charge

Phase 4 — Rinse and Passivation
1.  Drain acid to neutralisation tank (add soda ash to pH >6.5 before disposal)
2.  Three-pass water flush until pH >6.0
3.  Circulate 2% sodium nitrite solution for 1 hour to passivate metal surface

Phase 5 — Return to Service
1.  Remove temporary connections; re-install isolation valves
2.  Perform pressure test at 1.3× design pressure (hold 30 min — no leaks)
3.  Slowly return to service per normal startup; monitor outlet temperature trend

POST-CLEAN VERIFICATION
- Within 48h of return to service, record outlet temperature and compare to design curve
- Target: approach temperature within 5°C of design value
- If target not achieved, consider mechanical hydroblasting of tube bundle

RELATED DOCUMENTS
P&ID NPA-PID-009, E-301 Datasheet, COSHH-2020-E301-HCL, Thermal Performance Test SOP-MAINT-011

MAN-INST-001.title: Emerson Rosemount 3051 Pressure Transmitter — Troubleshooting Guide (Extract)
MAN-INST-001.content:
EMERSON ROSEMOUNT 3051 — TROUBLESHOOTING GUIDE (EXTRACT)
Document Reference: 00809-0100-4001 Rev EA

SYMPTOM: Transmitter reading high (positive drift) with no corresponding process change

Possible Causes and Diagnostics:

1. Fill Fluid Leak from Isolating Diaphragm
   - Description: A hairline crack or micro-fracture in the isolating diaphragm allows process fluid into the fill fluid cavity, or alternatively allows fill fluid to migrate into the impulse line.
   - Effect: Positive shift in reading (fill fluid loss causes diaphragm to deflect as if higher pressure is applied).
   - Diagnosis: Compare reading against a calibrated reference gauge or redundant transmitter. Inspect diaphragm face for visible pitting or deformation. Fill fluid (silicone or inert oil) may be visible at diaphragm face.
   - Action: Replace transmitter. Do NOT attempt to repair fill fluid loss in field. Return to OEM or authorised service centre.

2. Process Isolation Valve Partially Closed
   - Description: Isolation valve not fully open creates a partial restriction, causing transmitter to see dampened or offset pressure.
   - Diagnosis: Confirm isolation valve fully open (handwheel backed off 1/4 turn after full open).
   - Action: Open isolation valve fully.

3. Blocked Impulse Line
   - Description: Scaling, hydrate, or wax deposit in impulse tubing between process connection and transmitter.
   - Diagnosis: Pressure reading frozen or slow to respond to known process changes.
   - Action: Blow down impulse line per SOP-INST-005. If scaling suspected, use impulse line flushing procedure.

4. Zero Point Shift — Calibration Drift
   - Description: Electronic zero drift over time, typically <0.5% over 5 years. Larger drift suggests hardware issue.
   - Diagnosis: Compare to calibrator at known zero pressure. Use HART communicator to read sensor temperature — elevated temperature accelerates drift.
   - Action: Recalibrate using HART communicator if drift <1% of span. If drift >1%, replace transmitter.

5. H2S / Wet Gas Attack on Wetted Materials
   - Description: In H2S service, Hastelloy C-276 or 316L SS diaphragms can suffer stress corrosion cracking, particularly if process contains H2S + chloride + water at elevated temperature.
   - Inspection: At transmitter removal, inspect diaphragm face under 10× magnification for intergranular cracking or pitting.
   - Long-term Action: Specify Alloy 400 (Monel) or Alloy 625 wetted parts for H2S + chloride service. Reference NACE MR0175 material selection guide.

RELATED DOCUMENTS: 3051 Reference Manual 00809-0100-4001, NACE MR0175

SOP-SAFE-001.title: Emergency Shutdown System ESD-001 — ESD Activation Response Procedure
SOP-SAFE-001.content:
PURPOSE
Prescribe the immediate and recovery actions for Operations Technicians following activation of Platform Emergency Shutdown (ESD) on North Sea Platform Alpha.

SCOPE
All ESD levels: ESD Level 1 (Platform ESD), ESD Level 2 (Area ESD), ESD Level 3 (Equipment ESD). This procedure addresses Level 1 and Level 2 events.

ACTIVATION CAUSES (Common)
- Gas detector activation (fixed or portable) >20% LEL
- High-High pressure on protected equipment
- Fire detector activation (confirmed fire)
- Manual ESD activation (push-button on MCP or in field)
- Loss of utility power (partial or full)

IMMEDIATE ACTIONS — Control Room Operator
1.  Confirm ESD activation on DCS — identify initiating cause from first-up alarm
2.  Announce via PA system: "EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN ACTIVATED — ALL PERSONNEL TO MUSTER STATIONS"
3.  Confirm mustering complete via Talkback radio to all stations
4.  Do NOT attempt to reset ESD until cause is identified and cleared
5.  Notify OIM, Production Supervisor, and Maintenance Supervisor immediately
6.  Start ESD log — record all actions and times

IMMEDIATE ACTIONS — Field Operator
1.  Proceed to muster station unless assigned to an emergency response role
2.  If safe to do so and assigned, carry out visual inspection of ESD initiating area
3.  Report findings to Control Room by radio
4.  Do NOT re-enter gas-detected area without OIM authorisation and breathing apparatus

CAUSE INVESTIGATION
1.  Identify first-up alarm — Control Room operator to advise field team of suspected source
2.  Inspect for visible signs: gas cloud, liquid release, fire/smoke
3.  For gas detection: use portable gas detector to confirm/deny; identify source
4.  For high-pressure events: identify the overpressure origin from trend data

ESD RESET CRITERIA (All must be satisfied before reset)
□  Initiating cause identified and cleared
□  All personnel accounted for (muster complete)
□  OIM has authorised reset
□  Plant clear of hazardous concentrations (gas: <10% LEL; H2S: <1 ppm in work area)
□  All ESD isolation valves confirmed closed (visual confirmation in field)
□  Instrument air and power systems healthy

RESTART SEQUENCE
1.  Instrument air system first — confirm header pressure >7 barg before proceeding
2.  Utilities: Generators, cooling water
3.  HP Separation Train — follow SOP-OPS-001 for V-101
4.  LP Compression — follow SOP-OPS-010 for K-201/K-202
5.  Export — final step; confirm metering system healthy before opening export SDV-501

RELATED DOCUMENTS
ESD Cause & Effect Matrix NPA-ESD-CEF-001 Rev 12, SOP-OPS-001, SOP-OPS-010, Emergency Response Plan ERP-NPA-2023

SOP-OPS-001.title: HP Production Separator V-101 — Normal Startup Procedure
SOP-OPS-001.content:
PURPOSE
This procedure describes the steps required to bring HP Production Separator V-101 into service from a cold depressurised state.

SCOPE
Applicable to Operations Technicians on North Sea Platform Alpha. Requires OT Level 2 competency.

PREREQUISITES
- V-101 internal inspection completed and sign-off received (see WO record)
- All instrumentation calibration certificates valid and in loop folders
- Isolation certificates cleared; all blinds removed per Blind Register BLD-001-V101
- Process Safety Review Form PSR-OPS-001 signed by Permit Authority

PROCEDURE

Step 1 — Pre-startup Checks
1.1  Verify all instrument isolation valves open: PT-101A/B, LT-101A/B, TT-101A/B
1.2  Confirm safety valves PSV-101 in service (isolation valves open, locked)
1.3  Check all man-ways closed and torqued to flange specification
1.4  Confirm corrosion inhibitor injection running at 50 ppm dosing rate

Step 2 — Pressurisation
2.1  Crack open HP inlet SDV-101 manual bypass 10% to allow slow pressure equalisation
2.2  Monitor pressure rise on PT-101A; raise to 45 barg over minimum 15 minutes
2.3  Check for leaks on all flanges and instrumentation connections during pressurisation
2.4  Once pressure equalised, open SDV-101 fully and remove bypass

Step 3 — Level Establishment
3.1  Open oil outlet LCV-101 in manual at 0%; allow liquid to accumulate
3.2  Once LT-101A reads 30%, transfer LCV-101 to AUTO with SP=50%
3.3  Confirm PT-101A and PT-101B agree within 0.5 bar; if not, contact Instrument Tech

Step 4 — Gas Routing
4.1  Open gas outlet to LP Compression via SDV-201 (interlocked — confirm K-201 or K-202 running)
4.2  Confirm FT-101 (gas flow) reading stable

Step 5 — Normalisation
5.1  Complete "V-101 In Service" checklist and file in Control Room
5.2  Log startup in Operations Log with actual time in service
5.3  Notify Production Supervisor

EMERGENCY ACTIONS
- High-High pressure: SDV-101 will auto-close on PSHH-101. Do NOT attempt to override.
- High-High level: LCV-101 will open to 100%. If level continues rising, close HP inlet.

RELATED DOCUMENTS
SOP-OPS-002 (Normal Shutdown), SOP-MAINT-015 (V-101 Inspection), P&ID NPA-PID-001 Rev 7
